expectations.text: Creates the issue via POST /rest/api/3/issue with issuetype, priority, and project key in the request body

expectations.text: Assigns the issue to the sprint via POST /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{sprintId}/issue (not by setting a sprint custom field)

expectations.text: Sets priority using the correct JIRA API format: {"priority": {"name": "High"}}
expectations.evidence: Response uses CLI flag '--priority High', not REST API JSON format.

expectations.text: Sets issuetype using correct format: {"issuetype": {"name": "Bug"}}
expectations.evidence: Response uses CLI flag '--type Bug', not REST API JSON format.

expectations.text: Includes guidance on authentication (JIRA_TOKEN env var or Authorization header with Basic base64(email:token))
expectations.evidence: Response includes export JIRA_API_TOKEN and jira init --auth-type basic.

expectations.text: Steps are in correct order: (1) find sprint ID, (2) create issue, (3) assign to sprint
expectations.evidence: Steps follow correct order: find sprint -> create issue -> assign to sprint.

expectations.evidence: Response includes jira init --server https://mycompany.atlassian.net.

ANALYST: with_skill scores LOWER than without_skill (-10pp). Root cause: the skill guides toward the jira CLI tool which misses REST API assertions.

ANALYST: Eval 1 (blocked tickets) is the most discriminating -- without_skill 100% vs with_skill 67%. The skill's blocker-removal section focuses on label manipulation, not the JIRA issue link API (GET + DELETE issueLink).
ANALYST: Eval 0 (create ticket) skill leans on CLI commands. The skill's cli-reference.md promotes jira CLI which is valid but bypasses raw REST API knowledge tested by the assertions.
ANALYST: Eval 2 (close sprint) is the skill's best showing (86%). Main gap: POST vs PUT for sprint closure -- a single factual error in the workflow documentation.

ANALYST: Key fixes needed: (1) add issue link workflow (GET links, DELETE issueLink) to blocker resolution section in workflows.md, (2) fix sprint closure from POST to PUT in cli-reference.md and workflows.md, (3) ensure REST API curl examples appear alongside CLI alternatives in SKILL.md.
ANALYST: The CLI-first approach is a reasonable design choice, but the skill should surface raw API endpoints so users without jira CLI still get actionable guidance.
